\subsection*{6.2.3.3 AAI-RNG-ACK}

The AAI-RNG-ACK message is sent by the ABS in response to the CDMA ranging request during initial ranging, periodic ranging, and HO ranging in order to provide PHY -level adjustment (e.g., timing offset, power level, and frequency offset). In addition, it may also be transmitted asynchronously to send corrections based on measurements that have been made on other received data or MAC messages. As a result, the AMS shall be prepared to receive an AAI-RNG-ACK at any time, not just following a ranging preamble transmission. Upon reception of ranging preamble codes, the AAI-RNG-ACK message is transmitted by the ABS in a DL resource allocated by Broadcast assignment A-MAP IE. If ABS receives a dedicated ranging preamble code, the AAI-RNG-ACK message in response to the ranging preamble code may be allocated by DL basic assignment A-MAP IE with unicast STID. When the AAI-RNG-ACK message is sent unsolicitedly to a specific user, the AAI-RNG-ACK message is allocated by DL basic assignment A-MAP IE with unicast STID, and it shall not contain Number of frame identifiers, Frame identifier, RNG-ACK Bitmap, Number of received codes, and Ranging preamble code index. When the AMS receives an unsolicited AAI-RNG-ACK message, it shall reset the periodic ranging timer and adjust its PHY parameters as notified in the AAI-RNG-ACK message. Upon reception of ranging preamble codes, the broadcast AAI-RNG-ACK provides responses to all the successfully received and detected ranging preamble codes in all the ranging opportunities in a frame indicated by the frame identifier. If no ranging preamble code is detected in a ranging resource allocation for periodic ranging or initial/HO ranging, the AAI-RNG-ACK may be omitted. For initial ranging and HO ranging, if all the ranging statuses of the detected initial/HO ranging preambles are equal to "success" and no UL transmission parameter adjustments are needed, the AAI-RNG-ACK may be omitted by allocating UL resources with CDMA Allocation AMAP IEs or basic assignment A-MAP IEs for all the detected initial/HO ranging preambles. RNG-ACK Bitmap (starting from LSB) is encoded in ascending order based on the opportunity index.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-31-AAI-RNG-ACK message field description}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline If (transmitted in a DL resource allocated by Broadcast assignment A-MAP IE) \{ & & & \\
\hline For ( $i=0$; $i<$ N_Frame_Identifiers; $i++$ ) $\{$ & & The number of frame identifiers (i.e., N_Frame_Identifiers) [1..8] included in this RNG-ACK message & \\
\hline Frame identifier & 4 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Frame that contains the ranging opportunities to which this message refers. The frame identifier is produced by concatenating the following two values: \\
1) The 2 least significant bits of the superframe number. \\
2) The frame index within the superframe (ranging from 0b00 to 0b11).
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline RNG-ACK Bitmap & 4 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Each bit indicates the decoding status of the corresponding ranging opportunity. \\
0b0: No ranging code is detected \\
0b1: At least one code is detected
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline For each 0b1-bit in the RNGACK Bitmap \{ & & & \\
\hline For ( $j=0$; $j<\mathrm{N} \_$Received_Codes; $j++$ ) $\{$ & & The number of ranging preamble code indices (i.e., N_Received_Codes) [1..64] detected in this corresponding ranging opportunity. & \\
\hline Ranging preamble Code Index & 6 & Ranging preamble code index received in this ranging opportunity. & \\
\hline Ranging Status & 2 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Indicate whether ranging preamble code is received within acceptable limits by ABS . \\
0b00= success \\
0b01 = abort \\
0b10 = continue
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline If (Ranging Status == 0b01) \{ & & & \\
\hline Ranging Abort Timer & 16 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Timer defined by an ABS to prohibit the AMS from attempting network entry at this ABS, for a specific time duration. \\
Value: 0 (Do not try ranging again at the ABS.) \\
Value: 1-65 535, in units of seconds
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline \}else if (Ranging Status == 0b00|0b10) \{ & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-31-AAI-RNG-ACK message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Adjustment parameters indication (API) & 3 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Bit 0: Time offset adjustment indication \\
Bit 1: Power level adjustment indication \\
Bit 2: Frequency offset adjustment indication
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline If( API Bit 0==0b1 ) \{ & & & \\
\hline Timing offset adjustment & 15 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Amount of time required to adjust AMS transmission (in units of 1/Fs). \\
MSB 1 bit represents the sign of the value. That is, the value is negative $(-)$ if the $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 1$, and the value is positive ( + ) if the $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 0$. \\
LSB 14 bits represent timing offset correction value of [1..16384] that corresponds to $0 \times 0000 \sim 0 \times 3 \mathrm{FFF}$, respectively. \\
The AMS shall advance its transmission time if the value is negative (i.e., MSB = 0b1) and delay its transmission time if the value is positive (i.e., $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 0$ ).
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline If( API Bit 1==0b1 ) \{ & & & \\
\hline Power level adjustment & 4 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Power level adjustment that expresses the change in power level (in multiples of 1 dB ) that the AMS shall apply to its current transmission power for initial ranging. \\
MSB 1 bit represents the sign of the value. That is, the value is negative(-) if the $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 1$, and the value is positive( + ) if the $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 0$. \\
LSB 3 bits represent power level correction value of [1..8] that corresponds to $0 \mathrm{~b} 000 \sim 0 \mathrm{~b} 111$, respectively.
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline If (API Bit 2==0b1) \{ & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-31-AAI-RNG-ACK message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Frequency offset adjustment & 9 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Frequency offset adjustment. Relative change in transmission frequency. \\
The correction is $2 \%$ of the subcarrier spacing ( $f$ ) multiplied by the 9-bit number interpreted as a signed integer. \\
MSB 1 bit represents the sign of the value. That is, the value is negative(-) if the $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 1$, and the value is positive( + ) if the $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 0$. \\
LSB 8 bits represent a frequency offset correction value of [1..256] that corresponds to $0 \mathrm{x} 00 \sim 0 \mathrm{xFF}$, respectively.
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline \begin{tabular}{l}
\}//end of (Ranging Status \\
$==0 \mathrm{~b} 00 \mid 0 \mathrm{~b} 10$ )
\end{tabular} & & & \\
\hline \}//end of for & & & \\
\hline \}//end of for each & & & \\
\hline \}//end of for & & & \\
\hline \} else if (transmitted in a DL resource allocated by DL basic assignment A-MAP IE) \{ & & & \\
\hline Ranging Status & 2 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Indicate whether ranging preamble code or UL burst is received within acceptable limits by ABS. \\
0b00= success \\
0b01 = abort \\
0b10 = continue
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline If (Ranging Status == 0b01) \{ & & & \\
\hline Ranging Abort Timer & 16 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Timer defined by an ABS to prohibit the AMS from attempting network entry at this ABS, for a specific time duration. \\
Value: 0 (Do not try ranging again at the ABS .) \\
Value: 1-65 535, in units of seconds
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline \} else if (Ranging Status == 0b00|0b10) \{ & & & \\
\hline Adjustment parameters indication (API) & 3 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Bit 0: Time offset adjustment indication \\
Bit 1: Power level adjustment indication \\
Bit 2: Frequency offset adjustment indication
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\hline If( API Bit $0==0 \mathrm{~b} 1$ ) \{ & & & \\

Amount of time required to adjust AMS transmission (in units of 1/Fs). \\
MSB 1 bit represents the sign of the value. That is, the value is negative(-) if the $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 1$, and the value is positive( + ) if the MSB $=0 \mathrm{~b} 0$. \\
LSB 14 bits represent timing offset correction value of [1..16384] that corresponds to $0 \mathrm{x} 0000 \sim 0 \mathrm{x} 3 \mathrm{FFF}$, respectively. \\
The AMS shall advance its transmission time if the value is negative (i.e., $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 1$ ) and delay its transmission time if the value is positive (i.e., $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 0$ ).

MSB 1 bit represents the sign of the value. That is, the value is negative(-) if the $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 1$, and the value is positive( + ) if the $\mathrm{MSB}=0 \mathrm{~b} 0$. \\

\subsection*{6.2.3.4 AAI-RNG-CFM}

The AMS shall send the AAI-RNG-CFM message to the ABS upon successful ranging initiated by an unsolicited AAI-RNG-RSP message with the Ranging Request bit set to one. It shall include the following parameter:
- STID of the AMS

Additionally, the AAI- RNG-CFM message shall always include an MCEH with the polling bit set to 1 .

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-32-AAI-RNG-CFM message field description}
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|l|l|}
\hline Field & \begin{tabular}{c} 
Size \\
(bits)
\end{tabular} & \multicolumn{1}{|c|}{ Value/Description } & Condition \\
\hline STID & 12 & STID of the AMS & N/A \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection*{6.2.3.5 AAI-SBC-REQ}

An AAI-SBC-REQ message, to which the HARQ operation is applied, is transmitted by AMS to negotiate basic capability during network entry.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-33-AAI-SBC-REQ message field description}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline If (AMS requests transmittal of NSP information) \{ & & & \\
\hline SIQ (Service Information Query) & 2 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Bit 0: Indicates that the AMS requests transmittal of the NSP List for the list of NSP IDs supported by the Operator Network that includes the current ABS . \\
Bit 1: Indicates that the AMS requests transmittal of the Verbose NSP Name List in addition to the NSP List; bit 1 shall not be set to a value of ' 1 ' unless bit 0 is set to 1 .
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline \} else \{ & & & \\
\hline CAPABILITY_INDEX & 5 & It refers to the "Capability Class" that the AMS can support. Value: $0 \sim 31$. & \\
\hline DEVICE_CLASS & 5 & It refers to the "Device Class" that the AMS can support. Value: $0 \sim 31$. & \\
\hline CLC Request & variable & See Table 6-45. & Present if AMS requests to activate one Type I or II CLC class for fast CLC class activation during initial network entry. \\
\hline Long TTI for DL & 1 & If Bit $0=1$, it supports. & Present as needed. \\
\hline UL sounding & 2 & If Bit $0=1$, decimation separation based sounding (FDM) supports. If Bit $1=1$, cyclic shift separation based sounding (CDM) supports. & Present as needed. \\
\hline OL Region & 3 & If Bit $0=1$, OL Region type 0 supports. If Bit $1=1$, OL Region type 1, CDR and CoFIP supports. If Bit $2=1$, OL Region type 2 supports. & Present as needed. \\
\hline DL resource metric for FFR & 1 & If Bit $0=1$, it supports. & Present as needed. \\
\hline Max. Number of streams for SU-MIMO in DL MIMO & 3 & The number in the range 1 through 8 that is higher by 1 than this field. & Present as needed. \\
\hline Max. Number of streams for CL MU-MIMO (MIMO mode 4) in AMS point of view in DL MIMO & 1 & The number in the range 1 through 2 that is higher by 1 than this field. & Present as needed. \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-33-AAI-SBC-REQ message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline DL MIMO mode & 6 & \begin{tabular}{l}
If Bit $0=1$, mode0 supports. \\
If Bit $1=1$, mode 1 supports. \\
If Bit $2=1$, mode 2 supports. \\
If Bit $3=1$, mode 3 supports. \\
If Bit $4=1$, mode 4 supports. \\
If Bit $5=1$, mode 5 supports.
\end{tabular} & Present as needed. \\
\hline feedback support for DL & 11 & \begin{tabular}{l}
If Bit $0=1$, differential mode supports. \\
If Bit $1=1$, MIMO feedback mode 0 supports. \\
If Bit $2=1$, MIMO feedback mode 1 supports. \\
If Bit $3=1$, MIMO feedback mode 2 supports. \\
If Bit 4 = 1, MIMO feedback mode 3 supports. \\
If Bit 5 = 1, MIMO feedback mode 4 supports. \\
If Bit $6=1$, MIMO feedback mode 5 supports. \\
If Bit 7 = 1, MIMO feedback mode 6 supports. \\
If Bit $8=1$, MIMO feedback mode 7 supports. \\
If Bit $9=1$, Long-term reporting disabling support for MFM 0,4,7. If Bit $10=1$, Short-term reporting disabling support for MFM 2,3,5,6.
\end{tabular} & Present as needed. \\
\hline Subband assignment A-MAP IE support & 1 & If Bit $0=1, \mathrm{DL} / \mathrm{UL}$ Subband assignment A-MAP IE supports. & Present as needed. \\
\hline DL pilot pattern for MU MIMO & 2 & If Bit $0=1$, DL 4 stream pilot pattern for DL MU MIMO supports. If Bit $1=1$, DL 8 stream pilot pattern for DL MU MIMO supports. & Present as needed. \\
\hline Number of Tx antenna of AMS & 2 & The number in the range $\{1,2,4\}$ that is higher by 1 than this field. & Present as needed. \\
\hline Max. Number of streams for SU-MIMO in UL MIMO(1/2/3/ 4) & 2 & The number in the range 1 through 4 that is higher by 1 than this field. & Present as needed. \\
\hline Max. Number of streams for MU-MIMO in AMS point of view in UL MIMO(1/2/3/4) & 2 & The number in the range 1 through 4. & Present as needed. \\
\hline UL pilot pattern for MU MIMO & 3 & \begin{tabular}{l}
If Bit $0=1$, UL 2 stream pilot pattern supports. \\
If Bit $1=1$, UL 4 stream pilot pattern supports. \\
If Bit 2 = 1, UL 8 stream pilot pattern supports.
\end{tabular} & Present as needed. \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-33-AAI-SBC-REQ message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline UL MIMO mode & 5 & \begin{tabular}{l}
If Bit $0=1$, mode0 supports. \\
If Bit $1=1$, mode 1 supports. \\
If Bit $2=1$, mode 2 supports. \\
If Bit $3=1$, mode 3 supports. \\
If Bit $4=1$, mode 4 supports.
\end{tabular} & Present as needed. \\
\hline Modulation scheme & 2 & If Bit $0=1$, DL 64 QAM supports. If Bit $1=1$, UL 64 QAM supports. & Present as needed. \\
\hline UL HARQ buffering capability & 7 & Bit 0-6: The number that is higher by 1 than this field is the amount of information bits in 4800 bytes units the AMS can buffer in the UL. & Present as needed. \\
\hline DL HARQ buffering capability & 7 & Bit 0-6: The number that is higher by 1 than this field is the steady amount of aggregated DL HARQ information bits per frame in units of 4800 bytes, at which the aimed combining gain or better is obtained in the benchmark scenario, as defined in 6.2.14.2.1.3. & Present as needed. \\
\hline AMS DL processing capability per subframe & 7 & Bit 0-6: The number that is higher by 1 than this field is the steady amount of aggregated DL data information bits per subframe in units of 600 bytes that the AMS can process. & Present as needed. \\
\hline AMS UL processing capability per subframe & 7 & Bit 0-6: The number that is higher by 1 than this field is the steady amount of aggregated UL data information bits per subframe in units of 600 bytes that the AMS can process. & Present as needed. \\
\hline FFT size(2048/1024/512) & 3 & \begin{tabular}{l}
If Bit $0=1$, FFT 2048 supports. \\
If Bit $1=1$, FFT 1024 supports. \\
If Bit $2=1$, FFT 512 supports.
\end{tabular} & Present as needed. \\
\hline Authorization policy support & 1 & \begin{tabular}{l}
If Bit $0=0$, No authorization. \\
If Bit $0=1$, EAP-based authorization is supported.
\end{tabular} & Present as needed. \\
\hline Inter-RAT Operation Mode & 2 & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b00: single radio mode operation for inter RAT handover. \\
0b01: multi radio mode operation for inter RAT handover. 0b10-0b11: Reserved.
\end{tabular} & Present as needed. \\
\hline Supported Inter-RAT type & 8 & \begin{tabular}{l}
1 indicates support, 0 indicates not supported: \\
Bit 0: IEEE 802.11. \\
Bit 1: GERAN(GSM/GPRS/EGPRS). \\
Bit 2: UTRAN. \\
Bit 3: E-UTRAN. \\
Bit 4: CDMA 2000. \\
Bit 5-7: Reserved, set to zero.
\end{tabular} & Present as needed. \\
\hline MIH Capability Supported & 1 & If Bit $0=1$, the capability of IEEE 802.21 Media Independent Handover Services supports. & Present as needed. \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-33-AAI-SBC-REQ message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline MAX Tx Power & 24 & \begin{tabular}{l}
The maximum available power of the carrier for initial network entry. Bit 0-7: Maximum transmitted power for QPSK \\
Bit 8-15: Maximum transmitted power for 16-QAM \\
Bit 15-23: Maximum transmitted power for 64-QAM \\
Each unsigned 8-bit integer specifies the maximum transmitted power value in dBm . The maximum transmitted power is quantized in 0.5 dBm steps ranging from -64 dBm (encoded 0x00) to 63.5 dBm (encoded 0xFF). Values outside this range shall be assigned the closest extreme. If AMS does not support 64-QAM, the AMS shall report the value of 0x00 for Bit 15-23
\end{tabular} & Shall be present. \\
\hline If (ARS is a sender of AAI-SBC-REQ) \{ & & & //only available during ARS network entry phase. \\
\hline Relay mode & 1 & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b0: TTR relay mode \\
0b1: STR relay mode
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline if (Relay mode == 0b0)\{ & & & \\
\hline ARSTTG & 6 & ARSTTG value ( $\mu \mathrm{s}$ ). It shall be less than $50 \mu \mathrm{~s}$. & \\
\hline ARSRTG & 6 & ARSRTG value ( $\mu \mathrm{s}$ ). It shall be less than $50 \mu \mathrm{~s}$. & \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline Visited NSP ID & 24 & NSP ID of the Network Service Provider the AMS intends to be the conduit for authentication to the AMS home network. & Present as needed. \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

An AAI-SBC-RSP message, to which the HARQ operation is applied, is transmitted by ABS in response to a received AAI-SBC-REQ during initialization.

\hline \} & & & \\

\hline UL sounding & 2 & If Bit $0=1$, decimation separation based sounding (FDM) supports. If Bit $1=1$, cyclic shift separation based sounding (CDM) supports. & Present as needed. \\

Bit 3: E-UTRAN \\
Bit 4: CDMA 2000 \\
Bit 5-7: Reserved, set to zero
\end{tabular} & Present as needed. \\
\hline MIH Capability Supported & 1 & If Bit $0=1$, the capability of IEEE 802.21 Media Independent Handover Services supports. & Present as needed. \\
\hline Visited NSP Realm & variable ( $1 \ldots 128 \times 8$ ) & Visited NSP Realm. & Present if the Visited NSP ID is found in the AAI-SBC-REQ. \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection*{6.2.3.7 AAI-SON-ADV message}

This message is optional and used by an ABS connected to a core access network that supports SON (SelfOrganizing and Optimizing Networks) to broadcast relevant SON information for action types as defined in Table 6-35.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-35-Parameters for the AAI-SON-ADV message}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Action Type & 3 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Used to indicate the purpose of this message \\
0b000: ABS Reconfiguration \\
0b001: ABS Restart \\
0b010: ABS Scanning \\
0b011: ABS Reliability \\
0b100: LDM parameter change
\end{tabular} & Mandatory \\
\hline If (Action Type == 0b000) \{ & & & \\
\hline New IDcell & 10 & New IDcell that the ABS will use after the reconfiguration process & \\
\hline Unavailable Start Time (UST) & 8 & Start of unavailable time in unit of frame & Mandatory \\
\hline Unavailable Time Interval (UTI) & 8 & Interval of unavailable time in unit of superframe & Mandatory \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-35-Parameters for the AAI-SON-ADV message (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline else if (Action Type == 0b001) \{ & & & \\
\hline Unavailable Start Time (UST) & 8 & Start of unavailable time in unit of frame & Mandatory \\
\hline Unavailable Time Interval (UTI) & 8 & Interval of unavailable time in unit of superframe & Mandatory \\
\hline \} else if (Action Type == 0b010) \{ & & & \\
\hline Unavailable Start Time (UST) & 8 & Start of unavailable time in unit of frame & Mandatory \\
\hline Unavailable Time Interval (UTI) & 8 & Interval of unavailable time in unit of superframe & Mandatory \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline else if (Action Type == 0b011) \{ & & & \\
\hline Reason & 3 & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b000: Power down \\
0b001: Power reduction \\
0b010: FA change \\
0b011: Backhaul link down \\
0b100: Disable subframes
\end{tabular} & Mandatory \\
\hline If (Reason == 0b000) \{ & & & \\
\hline Time of Power Down & 8 & Expected time when the Femto ABS will be powered down in units of frame & Mandatory \\
\hline Expected uptime of BS & 8 & Expected uptime of BS in units of superframe & Optional \\
\hline \} else if (Reason == 0b001) \{ & & & \\
\hline Tx Power Reduction & 10 & dB value of Tx power reduction & Mandatory \\
\hline Expected time of power reduction & 8 & Expected resource adjustment time in units of frame & \\
\hline \} else if (Reason == 0b010) \{ & & & \\
\hline FA index & 8 & FA index & Mandatory \\
\hline Expected downtime of current FA & 8 & Expected current FA downtime in units of frame & \\
\hline Expected uptime of new FA & 8 & Expected uptime of new FA in units of superframe & Optional \\
\hline \} else if (Reason == 0b100) \{ & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-35-Parameters for the AAI-SON-ADV message (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Disabled subframes index & 6 & \begin{tabular}{l}
The status of DL subframes \\
Bit 0: The status of the 1st DL subframe \\
Bit 1: The status of the 2nd DL subframe \\
Bit 2: The status of the 3rd DL subframe \\
Bit 3: The status of the 4th DL subframe \\
Bit 4: The status of the 5th DL subframe \\
Bit 5: The status of the 6th DL subframe (0 means disabled or no such subframe; 1 means enabled)
\end{tabular} & Mandatory \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline else if (Acton Type == 0b100) \{ & & & \\
\hline LDM parameter-AI & 4 & The new AI for LDM & Mandatory \\
\hline LDM parameter-UAI & 8 & The new UAI for LDM & Mandatory \\
\hline LDM parameter-SFO & 9 & The new SFO for LDM & Mandatory \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline Recommended BSID list & variable & Recommended BSID list & It is optional to help AMS to HO \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection*{6.2.3.8 AAI-REG-REQ}

An AAI-REG-REQ message is transmitted by AMS to negotiate general AMS capabilities and to do registration during network entry.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-36-AAI-REG-REQ message field description}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline AMS MAC address & 48 & AMS's real MAC address. & \\
\hline MAXIMUM_ARQ_BUFFER_S IZE & 23 & ARQ buffer size depending on AMS's memory. Buffer size is defined as $2^{\mathrm{P}} (0 \leq \mathrm{P} \leq 23)$. Unit is byte. & Present if needed. \\
\hline MAXIMUM_NON_ARQ_BUF FER_SIZE & 23 & Non-ARQ buffer size depending on AMS's memory. Buffer size is defined as $2^{\mathrm{P}}(0 \leq \mathrm{P} \leq 23)$. Unit is byte. & Present if needed. \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-36-AAI-REG-REQ message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Multicarrier capabilities & 3 & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b000: No MC modes \\
0b001: Basic MC mode \\
0b010: Multicarrier Aggregation \\
0b011: Multicarrier Switching \\
0b100: Both Multicarrier Aggregation and Multicarrier Switching
\end{tabular} & Present if needed. \\
\hline Zone Switch Mode Support & 1 & Indicates whether the AMS has a capability to maintain its data communication with the ABS in LZone while performing network reentry in MZone 0b0: Not supported 0b1: Supported & Present if needed. \\
\hline If (LBS Capabilities are required to setup) \{ & & & \\
\hline Capability for supporting AGPS method & 1 & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b0: No support \\
0b1: Support
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline If (Interference mitigation is supported) \{ & & & \\
\hline DL PMI coordination capability & 1 & \begin{tabular}{l}
0: AMS is not DL PMI coordination capable \\
1: AMS is DL PMI coordination capable
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline DL collaborative multi-BS MIMO capability & 1 & \begin{tabular}{l}
0: AMS is not DL collaborative multiBS MIMO capable \\
1: AMS is DL collaborative multi-BS MIMO capable
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline DL closed-loop multi-BS macro-diversity capability & 1 & 0: AMS is not DL closed-loop multiBS macro-diversity capable 1: AMS is DL closed-loop multi-BS macro-diversity capable & \\
\hline UL PMI combination capability & 1 & \begin{tabular}{l}
0: AMS is not UL PMI combination capable \\
1: AMS is UL PMI combination capable
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline Multi_BS sounding calibration capability & 1 & \begin{tabular}{l}
0: AMS is not multi_BS sounding calibration capable \\
1: AMS is multi_BS sounding calibration capable
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-36-AAI-REG-REQ message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline E-MBS capabilities & 3 & \begin{tabular}{l}
If Bit 0 is set to 1 , it indicates E-MBS in S-ABS only is supported If Bit 1 is set to 1 , it indicates macrodiversity multi ABS E-MBS is supported \\
If Bit 2 is set to 1, it indicates non-macro-diversity multi ABS E-MBS is supported \\
If all Bit $0 \sim$ Bit 2 are set to 0 , it indicates no E-MBS is supported
\end{tabular} & Present if needed. \\
\hline Channel BW and Cyclic prefix & 15 & \begin{tabular}{l}
If Bit $0=1,5 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 16$ CP) \\
If Bit $1=1,5 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports (1/8 CP) If Bit 2 $=1,5 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 4 \mathrm{CP}$ ) \\
If Bit 3 $=1,10 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 16$ CP) \\
If Bit $4=1,10 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 8$ CP) \\
If Bit 5 $=1,10 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 4$ CP) \\
If Bit $6=1,20 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 16$ CP) \\
If Bit $7=1,20 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 8$ CP) \\
If Bit 8 $=1,20 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 4$ CP) \\
If Bit $9=1,8.75 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports (1/16 CP) \\
If Bit $10=1,8.75 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 8$ CP) \\
If Bit $11=1,8.75 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 4$ CP) \\
If Bit $12=1,7 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 16$ CP) \\
If Bit $13=1,7 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 8$ CP) \\
If Bit $14=1,7 \mathrm{MHz}$ supports ( $1 / 4$ CP)
\end{tabular} & Present if needed. \\
\hline Frame configuration to support R1 R1.0 & 4 & \begin{tabular}{l}
If Bit $0=1, \mathrm{R} 15 \mathrm{MHz}$ is supported (TDD only) \\
If Bit $1=1, \mathrm{R} 110 \mathrm{MHz}$ is supported (TDD only) \\
If Bit $2=1, \mathrm{R} 18.75 \mathrm{MHz}$ is supported (TDD only) \\
If Bit 3 $=1, \mathrm{R} 17 \mathrm{MHz}$ is supported (TDD only)
\end{tabular} & Present if needed. \\
\hline Persistent Allocation support & 1 & If Bit $0=1$, it supports & Present if needed. \\
\hline Group Resource Allocation support & 1 & If Bit $0=1$, it supports & Present if needed. \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-36-AAI-REG-REQ message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\

If Bit $0=1$, Type I CLC class support If Bit $1=1$, Type II CLC class subtype 1 \\
If Bit $2=1$, Type II CLC class subtype 2 \\
If Bit $3=1$, Type II CLC class subtype 3 \\
If Bit $4=1$, Type III CLC class
\end{tabular} & Present if needed. \\

Indicates whether the AMS has a capability to support EBB operation during handover \\
0b0: Not supported \\
0b1: Supported
\end{tabular} & Present if needed. \\
\hline If (EBB Handover support == 0b1) \{ & & & \\
\hline Minimal HO Reentry Interleaving Interval & 2 & The minimal HO_Reentry_Interleaving_Interval measured in frames. For AMS capable of Carrier Aggregation (Multicarrier Capability = 0b010 or 0b100), this value shall be 0 . & \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline Capability for sounding antenna switching support & 1 & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b0: No support \\
0b1: Support
\end{tabular} & Present if needed. \\
\hline If (Capability for sounding antenna switching support == 0b1) \{ & & & \\

0b0: Among DL Rx antennas \\
0b1: Among UL Tx antennas
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline \} & & & \\

\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-36-AAI-REG-REQ message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline CS type & 16 & \begin{tabular}{l}
A bit set to '1' indicates which CS Type the AMS supports \\
Bit 0: Reserved \\
Bit 1: Packet, IPv4 \\
Bit 2: Packet, IPv6 \\
Bit 3: Packet, IEEE 802.3/Ethernet ${ }^{\mathrm{a}}$ \\
Bit 4: Reserved \\
Bit 5: Reserved \\
Bit 6: Reserved \\
Bit 7: Reserved \\
Bit 8: Reserved \\
Bit 9: Reserved \\
Bit 10: Reserved \\
Bit 11: Reserved \\
Bit 12: Reserved \\
Bit 13: Reserved \\
Bit 14: Packet, IP ${ }^{\text {b }}$ \\
Bit 15: Multiprotocol flow, IPv4 or IPv6 Traffic \\
(a: Classifiers for IEEE 802.1Q \\
VLAN tags may be applied to service flows of this CS type) \\
(b: SDUs for service flows of this CS type may carry either IPv4 or IPv6 in the payload)
\end{tabular} & Present if needed. \\

Indicates the level of PHS support. \\

\end{tabular} & Present if needed. \\
\hline Host-Configuration-CapabilityIndicator IE & 1 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Indicates whether the AMS supports the capability of configuring host using the received parameters through the AAI-REG-RSP message. (One bit indicator) \\
0b0: Not supported \\
0b1: Support
\end{tabular} & Shall be present. \\
\hline \begin{tabular}{l}
Requested-Host-Configurations \\
IE
\end{tabular} & variable & Includes requested host configuration options in DHCP Options format. & Shall be present when Host-Configuration-Capability-Indicator IE is set by 0b1 and additional host configurations are required. \\
\hline Global carrier configuration change count & 3 & Indicates the AMS's last received value of Global carrier configuration change count of the network. If set to 0, it implies that AMS never received a Global carrier configuration information. & \\

\hline Vendor ID & 24 & Vendor identification specified by the 3-byte, vendor-specific organizationally unique identifier of the AMS or ABS MAC address. & Present to indicate the vendor ID of the AMS sending this message if vendor-specific information is used. \\
\hline Mobility features supported & 4 & \begin{tabular}{l}
A bit set to '1' indicates which Mobility feature the AMS supports Bit 0: Mobility (HO) support \\
Bit 1: Sleep mode support \\
Bit 2: DCR mode support \\
Bit 3: Reserved, this bit shall be ' 0 '
\end{tabular} & Present if the AMS supports at least one feature. \\

\subsection*{6.2.3.9 AAI-REG-RSP}

An AAI-REG-RSP message is transmitted by ABS in response to AAI- REG-REQ message during initialization.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-37-AAI-REG-RSP message field description}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline STID & 12 & AMS identifier that the ABS assigns to the AMS in place of the temporary STID that has been transferred by AAI-RNG-RSP message & Shall be included when an AMS is performing initial network entry or an AMS has no STID preassigned when it is performing network reentry procedure \\
\hline MAPMask Seed & 15 & The value of this parameter is the seed used at the ABS to initiate the PRBS generator used to scramble the 40-bit A-A-MAP IE when the value of the STID included in this message is used as the CRC Mask Masking Code. See 6.3.5.3.2.4 & Shall be present if STID is present \\
\hline CRID & 72 & AMS identifier that the AMS has been assigned for coverage loss or DCR mode & Shall be included when AMS is attaching in the advanced network mode (i.e., network configuration bit in SFH = 0b0) \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

0b0: No support \\
0b1: Support
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline \} & & & \\

0: ABS is not DL collaborative multiBS MIMO capable \\
1: ABS is DL collaborative multi-BS MIMO capable
\end{tabular} & \\

1: ABS is multi_BS sounding calibration capable

\hline

\subsection*{6.2.3.10 AAI-HO-IND}

The AMS may send the AAI-HO-IND MAC control message in HO preparation, HO execution, and HO cancellation. If Piggyback Extended Header is included in the AAI-HO-IND message, an aggregated bandwidth request size should be transferred to the T-ABS. If an incremental bandwidth request is piggybacked by the AMS, the Serving BS shall calculate the aggregated bandwidth request size and forward it to the T-ABS. The AAI-HO-IND message includes the information in Table 6-38.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-38-AAI-HO-IND message field description}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline HO Event Code & 2 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Used to distinguish AAI-HO-IND among different scenarios 0b00: T-ABS selection in case of multiple candidate T-ABSs in HO-CMD. The S-ABS does not meet the "ABS unreachable" triggers 0b01: All T-ABSs in AAI-HO-CMD are unreachable. The AMS includes a new T-ABS that was not included in AAI-HO-CMD. The S-ABS does not meet the "ABS unreachable" triggers 0b10: The S-ABS meets the "ABS unreachable" triggers. The AMS will attempt network reentry at the indicated T-ABS \\
0b11: HO cancel. The S-ABS does not meet the "ABS unreachable" triggers
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline If (HO Event Code == 0b00 or 0b01 or 0b10) \{ & & & \\
\hline T-ABS ID & 48 & & \\
\hline T-ABS Physical Carrier Index & 6 & Physical carrier index of the T-ABS where an AMS will perform network reentry procedure & May be included when the T-ABS is a multicarrier ABS \\
\hline AMS Physical carrier index at the S-ABS & 6 & Recommended carrier index of the AMS that was used in the S-ABS, and to be used for network reentry to the T-ABS. All other AMS's carriers that were used in the S-ABS can be used for data communication with the SABS & May be included when AMS's multicarrier capability = 0b10, HO Reentry Mode $=1$ and the target carrier is different from the serving carrier \\
\hline \}else if (HO Event Code == 0b11) \{ & & & \\
\hline SFH mismatch indication & 1 & 1: The AMS does not have the latest SFH for any of the T-ABSs included in the AAI-HO-CMD message; 0: Otherwise & \\
\hline AK_COUNT & 16 & The AMS's current value of the AK_COUNT & \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection*{6.2.3.11 AAI-HO-REQ}

In AMS-initiated HO, the AMS shall send the AAI-HO-REQ to the S-ABS to initiate the HO procedure. The parameters listed in Table 6-39 shall be included in the AAI-HO-REQ message.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-39-AAI-HO-REQ message field description}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\

\hline Carrier Preassignment Indication & 1 & Indicates whether AMS needs preassignment of secondary carriers at the T-ABS & May be included when AMS supports MC mode $=0 \mathrm{~b} 010$ or 0b011 or 0b100. \\
\hline for $(i=0 ; i<$ N_New_ABS_Index; $i++$ ) \{ & & N_New_ABS_Index is the number of neighboring ABSs to be considered for HO, which are included in AAI-NBR-ADV message. Range: $0 \sim 256$ & \\
\hline Neighbor_ABS_Index & 8 & ABS index corresponds to AAI-NBR-ADV & \\
\hline ABS CINR mean & 8 & CINR mean of the new ABS & May be included if it is available at the AMS. \\
\hline ABS RSSI mean & 8 & RSSI mean of the new ABS & May be included if it is available at the AMS. \\
\hline Physical_Carrier_Index & 6 & Physical carrier index of the ABS & May be included for each ABS with ABS Index or ABS ID when the ABS is multicarrier ABS . \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline for $(i=0 ; i<$ N_New_ABS_Full; i++) \{ & & N_New_ABS_ID is the number of neighboring ABSs to be are identified by full considered for handover, which is 48 -bit ABSID. Range: $0 \sim 256$ & \\
\hline Neighbor_ABS_ID & 48 & The 48-bit neighbor ABSID & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\hline ABS CINR mean & 8 & CINR mean of the new ABS & May be included if it is available at the AMS. \\
\hline ABS RSSI mean & 8 & RSSI mean of the new ABS & May be included if it is available at the AMS. \\
\hline Physical_Carrier_Index & 6 & Physical carrier index of the ABS & May be included for each ABS with ABS Index or ABS ID when the ABS is multicarrier ABS . \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline for $(i=0 ; i<$ N_R1_BS_Index; i++) \{ & & N_R1_ABS_Index is the number of neighboring R1 BS index to be considered for handover. Range: $0 \sim 255$ & \\
\hline Change Count & 3 & AAI-NBR-ADV Change Count & \\
\hline Neighbor BS Index & 8 & Indicates neighbor BS index index in AAI-NBR-ADV which contains the Change Count & \\
\hline BS CINR mean & 8 & CINR mean of the new BS & May be included if it is available at the MS. \\

\subsection*{6.2.3.12 AAI-HO-CMD}

The S-ABS shall send AAI-HO-CMD to initiate the HO procedure, or to acknowledge the AAI-HO-REQ sent by the AMS. The AAI-HO-CMD message shall include the parameters listed in Table 6-40.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\

0b00: HO command \\
0b01: Zone switch command from MZone to LZone 0b10: AMS HO request rejected (ABS in list unavailable). In this case, AAI-HO-CMD message shall not include any T-ABS 0b11: Reserved
\end{tabular} & N/A \\
\hline If (Mode == 0b00) \{ & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline HO Reentry Mode & 1 & 0b0: The AMS disconnects from the S-ABS before performing network reentry with the T-ABS 0b1: The AMS maintains communication with S-ABS while performing network reentry with the T-ABS & Shall not be set to 1 if the AMS does not support EBB (according to AAI-REG-REQ). \\
\hline If (HO Reentry Mode == 0b1) \{ & & & \\
\hline HO Reentry Interleaving Interval & 8 & If HO Reentry Interleaving Interval $>0$, the AMS performs network reentry to the T-ABS within the HO Reentry Interval and continues data transmission with the S-ABS during HO Reentry Interleaving Interval. If HO Reentry Interleaving Interval $=0$, the AMS performs multicarrier EBB (Entry Before Break) HO procedure per 6.2.8.2.9.2.2. & \\
\hline If(HO Reentry Interleaving Interval >0) \{ & & & \\
\hline HO Reentry Interval & 8 & AMS performs network reentry at the T-ABS during HO Reentry Interval. & \\

\hline \} & & & \\
\hline AMS Physical carrier index at the S-ABS & 6 & Recommended carrier index of the AMS that was used in the SABS, and to be used for network reentry to the T-ABS. All other AMS's carriers that was used in the S-ABS can be used for data communication with the S-ABS. & May be included when the AMS's multicarrier capability $=0 \mathrm{~b} 010$ or 0b100, and the target carrier is different from the serving carrier. \\
\hline \}//end of If (HO Reentry Mode == 0b1) & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-40-AAI-HO-CMD message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Disconnect time offset & 8 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Difference between Disconnect time and Action time in units of frames. The value of disconnect time shall be calculated for each T-ABS by adding/ subtracting this value with the value of Action time specified for this TABS . \\
For HO_Reentry_Mode = 0, Disconnect time will be (Action time - Disconnect time offset); For HO_Reentry_Mode = 1, Disconnect time will be (Action time + Disconnect time offset).
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline Resource_Retain_Time & 16 & The duration in units of 100 ms to which the T-ABS set the ABS-Resource-Retain-Timer. & Present if needed \\
\hline for $(i=0 ; i<$ N_Target_BS; $i++)$ \{ & & N_Target_BS is the number of T-ABSs or target R1 BSs included in this message. & \\

\hline SA-Preamble Index & 10 & Indicate the SA-Preamble index of the carrier. & Shall be included if the BS is T-ABS \\
\hline Preamble Index & 7 & Indicate the preamble index of the neighbor BS . & Shall be included if the BS is target R1 BS \\
\hline Center Frequency & 32 & Indicates center frequency (in unit of Hz) of the carrier. & Shall be included \\
\hline Action Time & 8 & The 8 least significant bits of the absolute frame number at the TABS where the AMS starts to perform network reentry. When CDMA_RNG_FLAG is set to 1 , it indicates the frame whereafter the AMS starts a CDMA ranging process. The action time should be set to a frame that includes either a nondynamic ranging channel or a dynamic ranging channel. When CDMA_RNG_FLAG is set to 0 , it indicates the frame where the AMS starts to expect the UL bandwidth allocation for transmission of RNG-REQ at target R1 BS or LZone (i.e., Fast ranging opportunity) or AAI-RNG-REQ at T-ABS. & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

0b0: 0b01 0b1: 0b10
\end{tabular} & Optional \\

\hline \} & & & \\

\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-40-AAI-HO-CMD message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Reentry process optimization & 5 & \begin{tabular}{l}
For each bit location, a value of 0 indicates the associated reentry MAC control messages shall be required, a value of 1 indicates the reentry MAC control message may be omitted. Bit 0: Omit AAI-SBC-REQ/RSP MAC control messages during reentry processing Bit 1: Omit PKM Authentication phase Bit 2: Omit AAI-REG-REQ/ RSP message during reentry processing \\
Bit 3: Omit higher layer protocol triggering for IP address refresh during reentry processing Bit 4: If Bit $4=1$, Full service and operational state transfer or sharing between Serving BS and Target BS (All static and dynamic context, e.g., ARQ windows, state machines.
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline Seamless HO & 1 & Indicates whether seamless HO is supported. & \\
\hline Ranging initiation deadline & 8 & An AMS shall send the AAI-RNG-REQ message during HO until Ranging initiation deadline. & Optional. Shall not be included if the BS is target R1 BS \\
\hline Preassigned MAPMask Key & 15 & The value of this parameter is the seed used at the T-ABS to initiate the PRBS generator used to scramble the 40-bit A-AMAP IE when the value of the STID included in this message is used as the CRC Mask Masking Code. See 6.3.5.3.2.4. & \\
\hline Service level prediction & 2 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Indicates the level of service the AMS can expect from this ABS. The following encodings apply: 0b00: No service possible for this AMS \\
0b01: Some service is available for one or several service flows authorized for the AMS 0b10: For each authorized service flow, a MAC connection can be established with QoS specified by the AuthorizedQoSParamSet 0b11: No service level prediction available
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection*{6.2.3.13 AAI-NBR-ADV}

The AAI-NBR-ADV message may sort neighbor ABSs (RSs) according to their deployment types, which is categorized by the following parameters:
a) Cell type (macro, micro, macro hot-zone, Femto, relay)
b) Physical carrier index referring the AAI-Global-CFG message that provides carrier frequency, BW, TDD/FDD, and related definitions (expected to be the same given carrier frequency)
c) MAC version

ABS determines and indicates the system configuration information included for each deployment type and their corresponding broadcast frequency.

To allow AAI-NBR-ADV fragmentation while providing flexibility for AMS HO operation without requiring acquisition of the whole AAI-NBR-ADV message, ABS always provides the total number of cell types, the total number of segments for each type, and the total number of neighbor ABS per segment. Each AAI-NBR-ADV fragment is indicated by the AAI-NBR-ADV Segment Index. Once an AAI-NBR-ADV segment is sent for a given cell type, the ABS should not start sending ABS for different cell types until all AAI-NBR-ADV segments for such given cell type are sent. ABSs with identical types are listed in the AAI-NBR-ADV message in descending order of their cell coverage.

Each AAI-NBR-ADV message carries the following:
- AAI-NBR-ADV change count
- Number of total cell types
- Segment information for this AAI-NBR-ADV message
- System information of ABSs of a specific cell type
- Starting ABS Index: Starting ABS Index of the AAI-NBR-ADV segment is the value of one incremented from the last ABS index of the previous AAI-NBR-ADV segment. The Starting ABS Index is the index of the first NBR ABS that is sent in a AAI-NBR-ADV segment. The index of the second NBR ABS is equal to Starting ABS Index +1 , and so on, so that all ABSs in this AAI-NBRADV segment have consecutive indices that are in ascending order. If this is the first AAI-NBRADV segment or the AAI-NBR-ADV message is not segmented, the Starting ABS Index will be 0 .

For each cell type, shared configuration parameters are carried as follows:
- Prefix of BSID
- SA-Preamble Index range
- Physical carrier range

The Prefix of BSID, SA-Preamble Index range, and Physical carrier range are optional fields, which can be used when the S-ABS chooses not to broadcast configuration information for each individual ABS within the cell type.

Within each cell type, if S-ABS chooses to broadcast configuration information for each individual ABS instead of specifying SA-Preamble Index range and Physical carrier range, a list of ABSs is provided and the following parameters are included for each ABS:
- 48-bit BS-ID
- ABS SA-Preamble Index
- Indication of whether full system information or partial information is carried for this ABS, which includes the following:
- SFH information
- Physical carrier index (6 bits, refer to the "physical carrier index" defined in AAI-Global-CFG)
- MAC protocol versions (8 bits)
- Paging carrier indication ( 1 bit, refer to specify if a carrier is a paging carrier or not)

For ABS of macrocell type, all the necessary system information shall be included, and the format may only carry delta information fields with respect to the reference ABS (e.g., the S-ABS or the preceding neighbor BS/ABS of this cell type); and for the Wireless-MAN-OFDMA reference system, only 48-bit BS-ID and Preamble index are included in AAI-NBR-ADV.
- SFH_encoding_format: Based on the present system information, the list of ABSs shall be categorized into three groups. The categorization is indicated by SFH_encoding_format parameter:
- 0b00: full Subpacket information
- 0b01: delta encoding with reference to the information of current carrier that transmits this AAI-NBR-ADV message
- 0b10: no SFH included
- 0b11: delta encoding with reference to the information of the preceding carrier

Dynamic SFH information such as the MSB of the superframe number shall not be transmitted in the AAI-NBR-ADV message.

The parameters NBR-ADV offset and NBR-ADV interval indicate the existence of neighbor R1 BS or LZone of ABS. The parameters shall be included in the AAI-NBR-ADV message when ABS indicates the existence of neighbor R1 BS or LZone of ABS.

NBR-ADV offset: The MOB_NBR-ADV to be scheduled after the frame $\mathrm{n}+$ NBR-ADV offset when the AAI-NBR-ADV message is sent in the frame n . Unit: Frame.

NBR-ADV interval: The interval of MOB_NBR-ADV is sent in LZone. Unit: Frame.

\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}

\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Change Count & 3 & AAI-NBR-ADV Change Count & \\
\hline Total number of cell types & 3 & The total number of cell types & \\
\hline Cell type & 3 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Cell type in this message \\
0b000: macro \\
0b001: micro \\
0b010: macro hot-zone \\
0b011: femto \\
0b100: TTR relay \\
0b101: R1 BS or LZone of ABS \\
0b110-0b111: Reserved
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline Total Number of AAI-NBRADV Segments & 4 & Total number of segments of AAI-NBR-ADV & \\

\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Starting ABS Index & 8 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Starting ABS Index is the index offset from the last ABS of the previous AAI-NBR-ADV segment. If this is the first AAI-NBR-ADV segment, the Starting ABS Index will be 0 . Hence, each AAI-NBR-ADV segment has one Index that corresponds to the first ABS in that AAI-NBRADV segment. \\
The order of the ABS within a segment serves as an offset from the 'Starting ABS index'. The sum of the offset and the 'Starting ABS index' serves as the ABS index that has the range of [1..256].
\end{tabular} & \\

\hline BSID & 48 & Neighbor ABS ID & \\
\hline MAC protocol version & 8 & MAC protocol version of the BS Consistent with IEEE Std 802.16 definition, with new MAC protocol version 10 defined for AAI. & \\
\hline CP time & 2 & \begin{tabular}{l}
CP time of the BS \\
0b00: 1/8 \\
0b01: 1/16 \\
0b10: 1/4
\end{tabular} & \\

\hline Physical carrier index & 6 & Refer to the physical carrier index in AAI-Global-CFG message & \\
\hline Paging carrier indication & 1 & Indicates whether the carrier is a paging carrier in ABS or not 0: no paging carrier 1: paging carrier & May be present when multiple carrier operation is applied. \\
\hline PGID & 16 & Indicates paging group identifier where the carrier belongs & \\
\hline S-SFH change count & 4 & Indicates the value of S-SFH change count of this neighbor ABS & \\

0b00: full Subpkt information 0b01: delta encoding with reference to the information of current carrier that transmits this AAI-NBR-ADV message 0b10: no SFH included 0b11: delta encoding with reference to the information of the preceding carrier \\

\hline \} & & & \\

\hline Neighbor BSID & 48 & Neighbor Base Station ID. & \\

A value of ' 0 ' indicates a reuse factor of 1 and a value of '1' indicates reuse factor of 3 Bit 6 to Bit 0: Represent the preamble index of the neighbor R1 BS & \\

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-41-AAI-NBR-ADV message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline PHY mode ID & 16 & Specifies the PHY parameters of each neighbor R1 BS, including channel bandwidth, FFT size, cyclic prefix, and frame duration, defined in 11.18.2 in IEEE Std 802.16. & \\
\hline Channel bandwidth & 2 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Channel bandwidth of the neighbor R1 BS. R1 BS uses 5 ms frame duration, 1/8 CP \\
0b00: 5 MHz \\
0b01: 7 MHz \\
0b10: 8.75 MHz \\
0b11: 10 MHz
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline R1 BS Center Frequency & 32 & Indicates center frequency (in unit of Hz ) of the neighbor R1 BS. & Present when S-ABS carrier index is different from that of target R1 BS \\
\hline DCD configuration change count & 8 & This represents the neighbor R1 BS' current DCD configuration change count. & May be present \\
\hline UCD configuration change count & 8 & This represents the neighbor R1 BS' current UCD configuration change count. & May be present \\
\hline if $(i==0)$ \{ & & & \\
\hline DCD_Info & variable & The information from 1st neighbor R1 BS' DCD message (Table 11-19 in IEEE Std 802.16). & May be present. If this parameter present, it is only for $1^{\text {st }}$ neighbor R1 BS \\
\hline UCD_Info & variable & The information from 1st neighbor R1 BS' UCD message (Table 11-12 in IEEE Std 802.16). & May be present. If this parameter present, it is only for $1^{\text {st }}$ neighbor R1 BS \\
\hline \} else \{ & & & \\
\hline Delta_DCD & variable & Delta encoding of DCD information (Table 11-19 in IEEE Std 802.16) that is different from the $1^{\text {st }}$ neighbor R1 BS . & Present if DCD information is different from that of $1^{\text {st }}$ neighbor R1 BS \\
\hline Delta_UCD & variable & Delta encoding of UCD information (Table 11-12 in IEEE Std 802.16) that is different from the $1^{\text {st }}$ neighbor R1 BS . & Present if UCD information is different from that of $1^{\text {st }}$ neighbor R1 BS \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline \}//end of for N-NBR-R1-BSs & & & \\
\hline $\operatorname{For}(i=1 ; i \leq$ N-PHY-CarrierIndices; $i++$ ) \{ & & N-PHY-Carrier-Indices is the number of PHY Carrier Indices listed here. (1..64). & \begin{tabular}{l}
Optional. \\
Present only for Cell type whose system info are not included in AAI-NBR-ADV
\end{tabular} \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}

\hline PHY Carrier Index & 6 & & \\
\hline IDCell range start & 10 & & Optional. Present only for Cell type whose system info are not included in AAI-NBR-ADV \\
\hline IDCell range end & 10 & & Optional. Present only for Cell type whose system info are not included in AAI-NBR-ADV \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline NBR-ADV offset & 4 & The frame number offset the MOB_NBR-ADV is sent from the start point of frame counting, in unit of frames. & Optional. Present only if the S-ABS is a Wireless-MAN-Advanced Air Interface co-existing System \\
\hline NBR-ADV interval & 4 & The interval of MOB_NBR-ADV being sent in LZone, in unit of frames. & Optional. Present only if the S-ABS is a Wireless-MAN-Advanced Air Interface co-existing System \\
\hline S-ABS LZone Preamble Index & 7 & S-ABS LZone Preamble Index for AMS to synchronize with S-ABS LZone to receive MOB_NBR-ADV. & Optional. Present only if the S-ABS is a Wireless-MAN-Advanced Air Interface co-existing System \\

\subsection*{6.2.3.14 AAI-SCN-REQ}

An AAI-SCN-REQ message is transmitted by an AMS to request a scanning interval for the purpose of seeking available ABSs and/or R1 BSs and determining their suitability as targets for HO. An AMS may request a scanning interval during the scan interleaving interval.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-42-AAI-SCN-REQ message field description}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Scan duration & 8 & Duration (in units of AAI subframes) of the requested scanning period. & \\
\hline Interleaving interval & 8 & The period of the AMS's active mode (in units of AAI subframes) that is interleaved between Scan Durations. & \\
\hline Scan Iteration & 6 & The requested number of iterating scanning interval by an AMS. & \\
\hline Recommended start super frame number & 6 & Represents the 6 least significant bits of the absolute super frame index for which the AMS recommends the first Scanning Interval to start. This field is set to 0 if an AMS has no preferred value. & Present if the AMS recommends a start super frame \\
\hline Recommended start frame index & 2 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Recommended start frame index within a super frame. \\
0b00: The 1st frame in a super frame 0b01: The 2nd frame in a super frame 0b10: The 3rd frame in a super frame 0b11: The 4th frame in a super frame
\end{tabular} & Present if the AMS recommends a start super frame \\
\hline N_Recommended_ABS & & \begin{tabular}{l}
This is the number of neighboring ABS the AMS plans to scan, which are included in AAI-NBR-ADV message [0..63]. \\
When an AMS receives AAI-SCNRSP message from ABS in response to AAI-SCN-REQ message, the AMS shall check whether Configuration Change Count stored by the AMS is the same as one included in AAI-SCN-RSP message sent by the ABS. If an AMS detects mismatch of Configuration Change Counts, it may retransmit AAI-SCN-REQ message to the ABS .
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline If(N_Recommended_ABS > 0) \{ & & & \\
\hline Configuration Change Count of AAI-NBR-ADV & 3 & The value of Configuration Change Count in AAI-NBR-ADV message used for neighbor ABS index references. & \\
\hline If(Nbr_Bitmap_Index is present) \{ & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-42-AAI-SCN-REQ message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Nbr_Bitmap_Index & variable [1..256] & \begin{tabular}{l}
Each bit position corresponds to a ABS Index of the corresponding AAI-NBR-ADV message, where the least significant bit corresponds to the first ABS Index, each next significant bit corresponds to the next ABS Index in sequential order, the most significant bit corresponds to the ABS Index of the last requested ABS , and ABSs with ABS Index greater than the last requested ABS are not requested and do not have a corresponding bit position in the bitmap. \\
Bitmap position bit value: \\
0 : The corresponding ABS is not requested. \\
1: The corresponding ABS is requested \\
Maximum size of the bitmap is 256 bits \\
Total number of '1's in the bitmap shall be equal to N_Recommended_ABS.
\end{tabular} & \\

\subsection*{6.2.3.15 AAI-SCN-RSP}

An AAI-SCN-RSP message shall be transmitted by the ABS either unsolicited or in response to an AAI-SCN-REQ message sent by an AMS. An ABS may transmit AAI-SCN-RSP to start AMS scan reporting with or without scanning interval allocation. If a Scan Duration field contains a nonzero value, the scanning interval pattern included in the AAI-SCN-RSP message replaces the previous existing scanning interval pattern.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-43-AAI-SCN-RSP message field description}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Scan duration & 8 & Duration (in units of AAI subframes) of the requested scanning period. & \\
\hline Report mode & 2 & \begin{tabular}{l}
0b00:No report \\
0b01:Periodic report \\
0b10:Event-triggered report \\
0b11:One-time scan report
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline Report metric & 4 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Bitmap indicator of trigger metrics that the S-ABS requests the AMS to report. The S-ABS shall indicate only the trigger metrics agreed during AAI-SBC-REQ/RSP negotiation. Each bit indicates whether reports will be initiated by trigger based on the corresponding metric: \\
Bit 0: ABS CINR mean \\
Bit 1: ABS RSSI mean \\
Bit 2: Relative delay \\
Bit 3: ABS RTD
\end{tabular} & \\
\hline Report period & 8 & The period of the AMS's report of scanning result when the AMS is required to report the value periodically or one-time. The period is calculated from the start of the first scan duration. If the ABS sends an unsolicited AAI-SCN-RSP message without assignment of a scanning interval and the scan duration is set to zero, the period is calculated from the frame the AMS receives the AAI-SCN-RSP message. For AMS scanning request denied by AAI-SCN-RSP with scan duration set to zero, Report period is the number of frames that ABS suggests to AMS before transmitting next AAI-SCN-REQ. & \\
\hline Duration for measurements signal location parameters on D-LBS zone & 4 & Duration (in units of 4 superframes) of the requested scanning. & Present if the ABS requests the AMS to scan the D-LBS zone for signal location parameters measurements \\
\hline If(Scan Duration > 0) \{ & & & \\
\hline Start superframe index & 6 & Represents the 6 least significant bits of the absolute superframe index in which the first scanning interval starts. & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

Bitmap position bit value: 0 : The corresponding ABS is not recommended 1: The corresponding ABS is recommended Maximum size of this bitmap is 256 Total number of '1's in the bitmap shall be equal to N_Recommended_ABS. & \\

\subsection*{6.2.3.16 AAI-SCN-REP}

When the report mode is 0 b 10 (i.e., event triggered) in the most recently received AAI-SCN-RSP, the MS shall transmit a AAI-SCN-REP message to report the scanning results to its S-ABS after each scanning period if the trigger condition is met. For a periodic report (i.e., Report Mode is 0b01) and for One-time Scan Report (Report Mode is 0b11), the AMS reports the scanning results to its S-ABS at the time indicated in the AAI-SCN-RSP message except when it is in the scanning interval. For a periodic report (i.e., Report Mode is 0 b 01 ), the AMS stops reporting after all scanning intervals in the AAI-SCN-RSP message. The AMS shall include all available scanning results for the requested ABSs specified in the said AAI-SCN-RSP message. The AMS may transmit an AAI-SCN-REP message to report the scanning results to its S-ABS at anytime or to get a unicast AAI-NBR-ADV with the system information for the requested cells (which are not already broadcasted by the ABS in the AAI-NBR-ADV message) and optionally the system information for other

Femto ABSs that are judged by the ABS to be in the vicinity of the AMS . The AMS may further indicate to the ABS for filtering the neighbor Femto ABS list based on the requested cell type by including "Neighbor_Request_BS_Type" indicator and/or the CSGIDs.

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-44-AAI-SCN-REP message field description}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline Report mode & 2 & Action code for an AMS's scan report of its measurement 00: Event-triggered report 01: Periodic report according to Scan report period of AAI-SCN-RSP 10: One-time scan report & \\
\hline $\operatorname{For}(i=0 ; i<\mathrm{N}$-Carriers-SABS; $i++)$ \{ & & N-Carriers-SABS is the number of carriers at the S-ABS to be reported in this message. & Present if the AMS reports measurement results measured from carriers at the S-ABS \\
\hline Carrier Index & 6 & Carrier Index of carrier at the S-ABS. & \\
\hline S-ABS CINR mean & 8 & The S-ABS CINR Mean parameter indicates the CINR measured by the AMS from the S-ABS. The value shall be interpreted as a signed byte with units of 0.5 dB . & Present if the AMS is asked to report CINR \\
\hline S-ABS RSSI mean & 8 & The S-ABS RSSI Mean parameter indicates the Received Signal Strength measured by the AMS from the S-ABS. The value shall be interpreted as an unsigned byte with units of 0.25 dB ; e.g., 0 x 00 is interpreted as -103.75 dBm . An AMS shall be able to report values in the -103.75 dBm to -40 dBm range. An AMS with multiple receive antennas will report the linear sum of the RSSI values calculated over all the receive antennas. & Present if the AMS is asked to report RSSI \\
\hline \} & & & \\
\hline S-ABS RTD & 8 & The S-ABS RTD parameter indicates the round-trip delay (RTD) measured by the AMS from the S-ABS. RTD can be given by the latest time advance taken by MS. The value shall be interpreted as an unsigned byte with units of 1/Fs. & Present if the AMS is asked to report RTD \\
\hline N_Reported_ABS & & Number of neighbor ABS reported in this message [0..63]. & Present only when the scanning report includes ABSs referred by ABS index \\
\hline If(N_Reported_ABS > 0) \{ & & & \\
\hline Configuration Change Count of AAI-NBR-ADV & 3 & The value of Configuration Change Count in AAI-NBR-ADV message used for neighbor ABS index references. & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 6-44-AAI-SCN-REP message field description (continued)}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Field & Size (bits) & Value/Description & Condition \\
\hline If (Rsp_Bitmap_Index is present) \{ & & & \\
\hline Rsp_Bitmap_Index & 8 & \begin{tabular}{l}
Each bit position corresponds to a ABS Index of the corresponding AAI-SCN-RSP message, where the least significant bit corresponds to the first ABS Index, each next significant bit corresponds to the next ABS Index in sequential order, the most significant bit corresponds to the ABS Index of the last requested ABS , and ABSs with ABS Index greater than the last requested ABS are not requested and do not have a corresponding bit position in the bitmap. \\
Bitmap position bit value: \\
0 : The corresponding ABS is not requested \\
1: The corresponding ABS is requested \\
Maximum size of the bitmap is 256 bits \\
Total number of '1's in the bitmap shall be equal to N_Reported_ABS.
\end{tabular} & \\
